TextENGNew: I had been taking it too easy on my day off — and before I knew it, I was running late.
Today was the day Rena and Mion were going to show me around Hinamizawa.
...And of course, my mom had to pick the exact moment I was heading out the door to dump an errand on me for the way back...
Rena and Mion were already waiting at the meeting spot.
"Kei-chan, you're late!"
"S-Sorry, sorry! Blame it on that special program last night — it was just too good to turn off!"
"Oh? That's your excuse for keeping two girls waiting?!"
"...Mii-chan just got here herself, you know."
"Ahahaha...☆ Last night's twenty-four-hour program was interesting, wasn't it?"
changeReason: New renders the program as 'twenty-four-hour program' matching JPN 密着24時間, while old invented 'A Day in the Life Of' not in the original.

TextENGNew: She was just as guilty as I was.
At least Mion seemed like she'd completely put yesterday behind her. ...Well, maybe she hadn't forgotten, but on the surface she was her usual self.
Still, maybe I should apologize, just to be safe? I was turning that over in my mind when Rena gently tugged my sleeve and gave a small, wry shake of her head.
It would be a shame to let a funny mood spoil what promised to be a great day... Yeah, best let it go.
...Setting all that aside, the next thing to catch my eye was the bag Rena had brought — a Boston bag that looked absurdly heavy.
changeReason: New specifies 'Boston bag' matching JPN ボストンバッグ, while old used generic 'handbag.'

TextENGNew: According to Rena, it was packed with lunch for our little picnic (?) today... Lunch!?
"D-Don't tell me that whole bag is crammed full of food?"
"Ahahaha~♪"
Rena just smiled in response to my question. Mion, in stark contrast, had already thrown up her hands in despair.
It's not that I dislike Rena's love of cooking, or her tendency to go all-out with everything... but seriously?
"...I-It wasn't any trouble at all... so don't worry about it, okay?☆"
Rena was carrying a Boston bag that looked incredibly heavy.
What on earth was in it?
Mion answered my unspoken question with a meaningful look.
changeReason: New corrects 'wink' to 'meaningful look,' matching JPN 目配せ (exchanging a knowing glance, not winking).

TextENGNew: That's right — Rena had actually gone and made a full spread of lunch.
"Kei-chan, after you left... apparently Rena went into overdrive!"
"It's not like I put her up to it!"
"...I-It really wasn't any trouble... so don't worry about it, okay?☆"
"She's been buzzing like that since last night! Can you take responsibility for this?!"
"A-Alright... I'm a man of my word. I'll take responsibility."
"...Huh... r-re... responsibility...!? What for?! What for?!"
The two of us slowly turned to look at Rena, then let our gazes drift down to that absurdly heavy Boston bag.
Common sense said it couldn't possibly be full of lunchboxes.
...But this was Rena we were talking about.
"...About two kilograms, maybe?"
"When Rena picked it up, I heard her grunt with the effort... I'd say five kilos."
changeReason: New captures JPN よっこらしょ (grunt when lifting heavy things) which old paraphrased as 'struggling a bit.'

TextENGNew: "N-No, that's too much! Keiichi-kun is a growing boy, so I made plenty... okay?☆ ...Ahaha... let's get going! ...Heave-ho!"
changeReason: New condenses significantly, dropping separate dialogue beats about Keiichi being a boy and Rena's explicit lines, which were distinct in the JPN with separate voice cues.

TextENGNew: The way she lifted it made it very hard to believe there were only lunchboxes inside.
"...Correction. I'd also say five kilos."
"I'll help carry it. But every last bit needs to be eaten! I won't forgive you if you make Rena sad, got it?!"
The only thing I could do for myself right now was exercise enough to work up a proper appetite...
After enough cheerful chatter, we set off at a leisurely pace.
A gentle stroll through soft morning sunlight...
It was the kind of healthy, wholesome thing I never could have imagined back when I lived in the city.
This was the deep countryside, after all.
Not a salaryman in sight, dragging himself to work on his day off.
changeReason: New uses 'salaryman' matching JPN サラリーマン, while old used 'slovenly desk jockeys' adding characterization not in the original.

TextENGNew: It really was a peaceful, quiet... wonderful place.
Small as the village was, you still ran into people just by walking around.
"Oh, good day."
"Good day to you. My, isn't that... Maebara-kun, I believe?"
The two of them greeted every single person we passed.
And every one of those people knew my name.
"How did I become this famous?!"
After the third person in a row turned out to know who I was, I couldn't help feeling a little unsettled.
"Ahaha, sad as it is... everyone knows everyone here because Hinamizawa has so few people."
"So basically... whenever an unfamiliar face walks by, everyone just assumes it must be that Maebara kid who moved here?"
"Yeah, that's about the size of it."
Humble as this process of elimination was... it was surprisingly effective.
I'd better be on my best behavior from here on out.
The day I'm spotted browsing idol photo books at the bookstore, I'd find myself branded a pervert before the next morning was out...!
Hinamizawa was not a place to be trifled with...!
And the horror didn't stop there.
"Of course I know them. The first one was old Takezou-san from the Makino bike shop. His hobbies are bonsai and playing the shakuhachi."
changeReason: New uses 'shakuhachi' matching JPN 尺八, while old generalized to 'flute.'

TextENGNew: "Next was Daisuke-kun, the second son from the dried-goods shop. His hobby is sharpshooting, and his dream is to become a top-class sniper."
changeReason: New corrects 'grocer's' to 'dried-goods shop' matching JPN 乾物屋, and 'ace sniper' to 'top-class sniper' matching 超A級スナイパー.

TextENGNew: "And the one just now was Miyo-san, the nurse from the Irie Clinic. Her hobbies are birdwatching and photography."
changeReason: New specifies 'Irie Clinic' matching JPN 入江診療所, while old just said 'the clinic.'

TextENGNew: "...You know the names of everyone we passed?... Their profiles too?!"
When I let my surprise show, Mion and Rena exchanged glances and laughed.
"Well, yeah. It's not like the city where neighbors hardly know each other."
"Let's put that to the test. You there — who am I?"
"Ahahaha. You're Keiichi Maebara. You say mean things sometimes, but deep down you're a kind, shy sort."
"You transferred here three weeks ago. Your hobby is napping. You recently switched to boxers. ...Didn't you?"
"That's quite enough!!"
"...B-Boxers..."
"Enough of that too!"
It seemed there was nothing you could keep private here.
changeReason: New says 'coming up soon' matching JPN もうすぐ, while old said 'On our next break' adding specific timing not in the original.

TextENGNew: Hinamizawa was truly not to be underestimated!
"This feels less like a tour for my benefit and more like showing me off."
"You're right."
"We've been parading around making quite a fuss, after all. It lets everyone see for themselves — that Kei-chan is settling right in here in Hinamizawa!"
"Hinamizawa is losing population. So the whole village is happy to welcome anyone new."
I almost laughed it off, but stopped myself.
Had I ever once greeted a newcomer who had moved into my old neighborhood in the city...?
When I thought about it that way, what they were saying didn't sound like a joke at all.
We passed another person.
And, sure enough, we were called out to again.
"My, hello there. How lovely to see you all getting along so well!"
"That's Mrs. Fujishima. Good day～!"
"Oh my, Maebara-kun! How lucky you are, with a girl on each arm! How are you settling in?"
I swallowed the polished, city-bred small talk I would have instinctively reached for, and answered with a firm nod instead.
The woman laughed and said she liked how energetic I was.
"Good!"
When I glanced back, Mion and Rena gave me a wink.
"...And so now, shall we maybe think about lunch? ...Maybe?"
Rena's brilliant smile announced what both Mion and I had been trying not to think about.
The two of us looked at each other.
"...I'm a man. I'll do my best. But there's just too much of it!"
"Leave it to me, Kei-chan."
...Mion had never seemed quite so dependable.
She was the class president for a reason!
"Rena, if we're going to eat, why don't we find somewhere with a nice view?"
"...Oh! Yes! That sounds wonderful. I agree～!"
Rena nodded happily at Mion's suggestion.
At the top of the stone steps, there stood exactly the kind of shrine I'd imagined.
It had weathered the years, but the fallen leaves had been swept clean, and the whole place had a tidy, pleasant feel.
"This is the Furude Shrine. It's probably the spot with the best view around!"
"Make sure you remember this place! There's a festival here coming up soon."
"Oh really. Isn't it a little early in the season for a festival?"
changeReason: New corrects 'temple' to 'shrine,' matching JPN 神社 (Shinto shrine, not Buddhist temple).

TextENGNew: "The Watanagashi isn't a summer festival. Apparently it started long ago as a celebration for the end of winter."
I felt a little embarrassed for having assumed all festivals had to happen in summer.
"Now then... let's spread out the lunchboxes... there..."
One colorful lunchbox after another was laid out across the picnic sheet.
The smell was undeniably good. It was Rena's cooking. It was absolutely going to be delicious.
...But was it actually possible to finish all of this?!
A nice view alone wasn't going to cut it, Mion!
"...Good afternoon."
changeReason: New reframes from prohibition to claiming ownership. Also 'Hamburg steak' vs 'hamburger' is more accurate to JPN ハンバーグ.

TextENGNew: It was Rika-chan and Satoko.
...Why were they here?!
Mion shot me a sly grin.
Oh — her secret plan was to overwhelm us with numbers...!
Bless you, Mion!
Once the stage was set, the rest was my specialty!
"We heard quite a commotion, so we came to look... What on earth is all this?!"
"Take a look. It's lunchtime. Buffet-style. And smorgasbord of Rena's homemade cooking."
"I can see that much!! Why have you laid out a mat on someone's property?!"
"A shrine is a public place. It doesn't belong to you."
"...Keiichi is quite right. It's everyone's garden."
"Rika-chan really is such a good girl...!! Come, sit down! Eat with us!"
After making room for Rika-chan, I promptly turned my back on Satoko.
"Hold on just a moment! Where am I supposed to sit?!"
"There's no seat for you and nothing for you to eat."
"...D-Don't worry, there's plenty for Satoko-chan too..."
"There isn't! I'll eat her share too!!"
"That is absolutely unacceptable!!! Rika!"
"...Here, chopsticks."
Satoko and I both lunged at the lunchboxes simultaneously.
"My word, Kei-chan, you have a real gift for winding people up. You might actually be talented at this."
"Have a plate. One for Mii-chan and Rika-chan too."
Rena deftly produced paper plates and chopsticks.
"...If we don't hurry, it'll all be gone."
"You're right. Alright then! Shall we enter the fray?!"
"Eat as much as you like! There's plenty for everyone.☆"
Rena said this as she unscrewed the cap from the thermos.
It dawned on me, only now, that this lunch had been made with five people in mind.
The quantity was still staggering regardless, but the meaning behind it was something else entirely.
"That Hamburg steak is MINE!!"
"Ow!! Elbows are against the rules, Satoko!!"
"Grabbing the back of my collar is also against the rules!!!"
It was a fierce contest. In the opening stages, Satoko had the upper hand with her momentum and elbow strikes, but the decisive gap in chopstick skill suddenly flipped the whole battle around!
"Satoko Houjou is VANQUISHED!!! NOM!!!!"
"Waaahhhh!!!"
Satoko and I managed to choke at the exact same moment, toppling over and flailing.
Even so, Rika-chan patted me gently on the head — though the food was very much lodged in my throat, not on it.
Watching that, Rena flushed red and began making little exclamations of rapturous delight.
Mion rebuked Rena for saying something that was bordering on criminal.
This was what mealtime always looked like for us.
If this was how things would keep going... I would spare no effort to make sure they did.
Satoko vigorously raised her arm and assumed an elbow chop stance.
changeReason: Old describes 'battle calmed and people talking' but JPN describes Satoko raising her arm for an elbow chop — completely different content, likely a misaligned entry.

TextENGNew: I sensed the movement behind me and spun around to block it — or at least, that was the plan.
"—Whoa?!"
Satoko's charge was so sharp that I reflexively arched my torso back and slipped aside.
"What?! ...Kyaaaahhhh??!!"
"Kyaaaaaah?!"
CRASH, BANG, CLATTER!!!!!
Faced with the sight of this unexpected catastrophe, we all froze in place.
...The damage was limited to a single lunchbox, but Satoko had caught the full blast of it square in the face.
"...Miip. Are you alright?"
In the silence, Rika-chan slowly extracted herself from her own frozen state and reached out to pat Satoko on the shoulder — the very shoulder of the girl who had her face buried in a lunchbox.
That shoulder was... trembling in tiny shivers.
"...W-Waaaaaah!! Keiichi is an absolute villain!!"
"—Wait, me?! This is somehow my fault?!"
"Well... thinking about it objectively and rationally, it might be a hundred percent your fault."
"That is completely subjective!! —Nngh?!"
"Hau~! The evildoer who makes Satoko cry shall be punished by Rena, in the name of the moon!"
"H-How does the moon enter into this...? ...Ugh."
"Poor Keiichi, falsely accused and punished. Very pitiful. Nipah~♪"
Hey, Rika! If you know it's a false accusation, then defend me! Why am I the only one getting ganged up on?!
...And so the picnic held in the shrine precincts grew lively and loud.
Satoko suddenly flung her arm up into elbow-strike position.
I sensed a presence behind me and spun around to block — or at least, that had been my intention.
changeReason: Complete meaning change: old describes the battle calming, while new (matching JPN 勢いよく沙都子は腕を振り上げ) describes Satoko raising her arm for an elbow strike.

TextENGNew: "—What?! ...Gugh?!"
Satoko's charge was so fierce that it blew right through my crossed arms, and her small but pointed elbow drove squarely between my eyes with a resounding crack!!
changeReason: Old adds 'I'm not sure what's going on' not in JPN. New accurately captures 眉間をごすッ (between the eyes with a crack).

TextENGNew: "K-Keiichi-kun?!"
"Oh dear... his eyes have completely rolled back. That was a direct hit to the vitals."
"...A critical strike."
"What?! ...A-Are you alright?!"
Satoko clearly hadn't expected the hit to land so cleanly either, and she rushed over to me in a panic.
She tapped me on the cheek, but I had gone down so completely that her voice didn't reach me at all.
"Sa-Satoko-chan... That was... I think that was a little too much..."
"I-I didn't... I honestly didn't expect it to connect that perfectly, you know?"
"...He shouldn't have turned around. Always trying to look cool... honestly."
"What do we do now? Personally, I'm quite pleased — there's more lunchbox for the rest of us."
"...No, at least wait until Kei-chan wakes up before we pick up the pace. ...And Rika-chan, stop poking Kei-chan with that twig!"
"Nipah~☆"
...I regained consciousness roughly ten minutes later. Most of the best side dishes had already vanished...
The furious battle finally settled down, and at last there was room for conversation.
I accepted some tea from the thermos and let out a slow breath.
"You know, why are there so few words in Japanese to properly praise how something tastes?!"
"Probably because the whole idea of leisurely conversation during a meal didn't really take hold until modern times."
"They say people in the old days ate in silence. ...That must have been lonely for whoever did the cooking."
"Back then, everyone was surely too busy eating to spare a word for compliments!"
That's just you.
"But you know, even a single word of praise makes me happy. It makes me feel like all my effort was worthwhile."
As she said it, the faintest blush rose to her cheeks.
"...It was delicious."
With perfect timing, Rika-chan's "praise" landed on Rena with pinpoint accuracy.
Honest, clear eyes. An expression of innocent guilelessness that was enough to devastate Rena.
Following that unintelligible sound, a ring of smoke rose from Rena's head with an audible pop.
"Hau~!! ...Ri... Ri-Ri... Rika-chan, I want to take you h— no, I mean! Thank you~~~~!!!!"
"...It truly was delicious."
Rena flushed bright red with excitement and pulled Rika-chan into a hug, rubbing their cheeks together so vigorously you could almost hear it.
"R-Rika-chan, as a reward for your kind words... ta-da! These are just for you!"
She stabbed two apple-bunny sculptures with toothpicks and thrust them toward Rika-chan.
The moment Rika-chan accepted them, the mood in the group began sliding in a strange direction.
"...What is it, Satoko? That's a very defiant look."
"Has everyone here prepared praise worthy of receiving one of Rena-san's apple bunnies?"
"Bold of you to say. What kind of compliment are you going to manage with your limited vocabulary?!"
"Hohoho... allow me to demonstrate."
After shooting Mion and me another defiant glare, Satoko suddenly shifted her tone of voice entirely.
"U-Um, hey! ...You know... Rena-oneechan's lunch was really yummy for Satoko too..."
Satoko looked up with wide eyes and spoke in a childish, lisping voice.
What! ...How is that praise?! That's not persuasion by tears — that's persuasion by sheer cuteness!
But it worked like a charm on Rena.
She turned red and her head began spinning in circles!
D-Don't fall for it, Renaaaa!!!!
"...H-Hauuuu~!!"
Asking for the impossible, of course. Rena threw herself at Satoko and began rubbing their cheeks together.
"Kyuuuute! ...Satoko-chan is so kyuuute too... ☆ ...take home...hau!"
Swish, stab! Poke! Satoko was also presented with her own apple bunnies.
After stuffing them into her mouth, she turned her defiant gaze on Mion and me once more.
Rena had been taken down in exactly five seconds.
H-How dirty! Was that kind of tactic even allowed?!
"If it bothers you that much, prepare some praise worthy of a reward from Rena-san!"
Daaaaaamnnn!! What a rotten trick!!
That wasn't even praise to begin with!!!
"Satoko. You think you've won with that? Let me show you something in a different league from your little shallow games!?"
"T-That sounds promising, Mion! What's the plan?!"
"Kei-chan is the one who does it."
"Excuse me?!"
"Hohoho! By all means, show me what you've got — the desperate flailing of an old maid!!"
...The strategy Mion proposed was ruthless.
But it was the only path to victory against Satoko!!
I set down my tea and spoke with perfect ease and calm. Flawlessly.
"...It's really good, isn't it. Was all of this made fresh?"
"Ah, no... actually, most of it is frozen stuff..."
"Then which part did you make yourself, Rena?"
"...Eh... uh? ...Umm... well... it's, um... a little embarrassing... do I really have to say? ...Do I?"
The combination of a boy asking about her cooking was sending Rena into a dreamy trance.
"...I know. It was this one, wasn't it?"
Rena turned as red as she possibly could, wearing an expression that said she couldn't believe it.
"H-How could you possibly know?! ...How?! ...Hau... T-The things Rena made herself..."
Of course, Mion had told me in advance.
...So far, this was perfect!!
Here I feigned a bashful pause and held it for exactly one heartbeat. ...Now for the finishing blow.
"...It smelled like you, Rena."
Silence.
...Rena stood perfectly still, face still completely flushed, unable to so much as move.
With a small yelp, Satoko went red too. ...And of course, so did I...
"...I'd really like to try one of Rena's handmade apple bunnies... or something..."
Even if this was all just for the sake of competing against Satoko... I was pretty sure I'd overstepped the line by about ten meters...
...Then, at that moment,
WHOOOOOOSH!!
It was a Tupperware container packed full of apple bunnies.
"P-P-P-Please eat some, Keiichi-kun?! Th-Th-There's plenty, plenty, plentyyy...!!"
HWURK?! In an instant, a dozen apple slices were crammed into my mouth and I keeled over.
"O-Okay, say ahhhh, K-K-K-K-Keiichi-kun... K-K-K-K-K... ☆"
My head was forcibly deposited on her lap, and more apple bunnies kept getting pushed into my mouth.
While she was making squealing sounds, one apple after another into my... mouth... one after... another... ap... ple...
``@bHow about that, Satoko?.@<H-Houthath Hathoko@>... @bLooks like.@<Hookth hike@>... @bwe win.@<hee hin@>...``
changeReason: Different localization of Keiichi's garbled speech: 'How bout them apples' vs 'How about that' — both convey taunting Satoko with mouth full; the phonetic garbling differs but the scene meaning is the same.

TextENGNew: "Kei-chan, what a magnificent way to go!! How's that, Satoko?! A complete victory for our side?!"
"I can't believe you pulled this... th-this doesn't upset me in the slightest!!!!"
Satoko was grinding her teeth in frustration! We did it!!
I confirmed my victory as consciousness slowly faded away.
...Just then, Rena seemed to notice something and stopped her squealing.
"...Rika-chan, you're not eating? ...Was the salt water too strong...? ...maybe?"
Looking over, Rika-chan had pulled the toothpick out of her apple bunny and was cradling it in her cupped hands with a puzzled expression.
"...The bunny looks... sad. ...I want to... save him."
Pfft!
...That was the sound of Rena's nosebleed.
"...K-Ky... Kyyyute~... Tonight for sure... I'll take you home... ☆"
She shook and shivered, her head wobbling round and round.
Then, coming back to herself, she gathered up all the apple bunnies around me in a rapid swoop and arranged them neatly on a plate.
"Now they're all together and no one's lonely...! Here, I'll give these to Rika-chan... okay? ...Okay!"
Rika-chan, having been handed the plate of bunnies, raised Satoko's hand like a referee, and murmured softly.
"...We win."
"...Wh-What... WHAAAAAT!?!? A comeback defeat!?!"
"...I... hust hont care heniho..."
...And so... my glorious sacrifice... had gone to waste...
I drained the last of my tea and looked directly into Rena's eyes.
"Thank you, Rena. It was wonderful. Honestly? This is the most delicious packed lunch I've ever had in my life."

"The richness of the ingredients spreads through your mouth with every bite. But more than that — what really fills your heart is the love Rena poured into each and every dish. I can absolutely tell how much heart you put into making this."
Rena flushed and went quiet.
"Ah, well... actually... most of it was frozen..."
"This is frozen...?!"
"I'm sorry... I'm so sorry! Rena wanted to make a lot, but there wasn't time to make it all by hand..."
...She said they weren't all handmade?! I didn't see that coming!
But the likes of me don't fold in the face of a revelation like that!
Rena was looking confused, her eyes shining with unshed tears. I drew those words around her like a warm embrace and pressed on with conviction.
"Frozen or not — it doesn't matter in the slightest! Absolutely no problem at all! No problem whatsoever!"
She tilted her head at me with that adorable little expression, like she didn't quite understand what I was saying. Like a squirrel, or some other small creature, blinking its wide, round eyes.

"Rena... What matters is sincerity. The taste of food comes from the heart. If you put your whole heart into it, then even frozen ingredients can produce dishes no top chef in the world could ever match..."
Satoko, Rika-chan, and even Mion were listening to me with their mouths hanging open.
Was I... making a really impassioned speech? But once my mouth started finding its stride, there was no stopping it now!
"No matter how fine the ingredients, no matter how brilliant the technique — nothing compares to this lunch. I can say it with certainty! No one can hold a candle to Rena!"
"Because no one else in the world could pour this much sincerity into a single lunchbox!"
changeReason: New uses 'ten meters' matching JPN 10m, while old converted to '9 yards' changing the cultural reference.

TextENGNew: Mion nodded with a solemn look. Even Satoko and Rika-chan were dazedly nodding along, as if they'd been gently swept up in it without knowing.

Rena's face was utterly aglow, little wisps of smoke rising from the top of her head. But I wasn't done yet! This was where Keiichi Maebara truly hit his stride! Push and push and push all the way to the climax...!
"Of course, a flawless sense for seasoning and presentation goes without saying — as does the balance among a rich and varied spread of dishes, all assembled with a discerning palate."
"But that's a given! For someone of Rena's caliber to even be questioned on such things — that would be the height of vulgarity. Like exposing your own ignorance for all to see!"

"So what is the decisive difference between this and some ordinary lunchbox?! It's the character of the person who made it. Rena's character is the essential spice — the very essence — that makes the finest food possible!"
changeReason: Old has garbled grammar 'Rena squirms looks at me' and misses JPN 盗み見ていた (secretly watching); new captures the furtive observation.

TextENGNew: "The gentle nature that seeps through without her even trying — that is what fills the hearts of everyone who shares in her food with warmth! There is no one else who could make something this extraordinary, and that someone is Rena!"
"...Eh... hm? ...Um... well... this is... a little... embarrassing..."
changeReason: Old correctly captures 'drank it down in one gulp' matching JPN ガッと一気に飲み干した, but new removes the gulping, only saying 'lifted to my mouth.'

TextENGNew: Even as she said it, Rena was falling into a state of bliss beneath the spell of my words. I feigned one last bashful pause and held it for exactly one beat. Then, deliberately...
"That's why..."
...Here comes the finishing blow!
"Rena is absolutely going to make a wonderful wife someday...!"
KABOOOOOOM!!
"Huh? W-Wife... whose...? ...Whose?...@k H-H-H-Hau hau hauuuu~~~?!"

A pop sounded above Rena's bright red head and a little ring floated up as she reeled back so hard she nearly fainted. Then, with a tremendous crash, she thrust a Tupperware container brimming with apples right at me.
changeReason: Old opens with fabricated content ('I'm sure you'll be able to guess') not in JPN, and garbles Rena's comedic physical reaction (pop sound, floating ring, reeling back).

TextENGNew: ...This match is mine!!
Satoko snapped back to her senses and frantically signaled to Rika-chan. Seizing the moment with immaculate timing, Rika-chan preempted me and spoke first.
"If I were a boy... then I would be the one to make Rena my bride."
R-Rika-chan?! My devastating final line — stolen!!
"Huh? ...H-H-H-Hauu hau hauuuuu~!! R-R-R-R-R-Rika-chan, I'm taking you home~~~!!!"
The Tupperware container full of apple bunnies that had been destined for me flew straight into Rika-chan's arms instead.
Wait...?! Seriously?! What kind of turn is this?! After all those embarrassing lines I delivered, the grand finale just got completely stolen at the last second?!
"Hauuu~~, Rika-chan, Rika-chan!@k What a cute little groom you'd make! You would, you would!!"
Rena flushed deep red and, squealing with excitement, hugged Rika-chan and rubbed their cheeks together. Watching it unfold, Satoko's face broke into a triumphant grin.
"Ohohoho! You got exactly what you deserved! I'm afraid this match goes to—"
"—Oh, but..."
Rena glanced up at me. ...What?
"What made me the happiest was Keiichi-kun's compliment. So, Keiichi-kun wins this match!"
Cheeks still faintly flushed, Rena held out a single apple bunny to me with quiet dignity.
Oh... OHHHH?! Heaven is on my side!!
"W-W-W-What?! I won't accept this!"
"Mii... ...Satoko and I lost on the comeback... "
"I-I reject that line, I absolutely reject it, I reject it with everything I have!!"
Rika-chan's shoulders drooped in disappointment. The fuming Satoko was waving her arms in a big X and yelling something, but naturally I ignored her.
changeReason: Old uses nonsensical 'battening her arms' for バッテン (making an X-shape with arms). The gesture meaning 'no/wrong' is lost, but the general context of Satoko protesting with her arms is partially preserved.

TextENGNew: "Ahahaha! That's our Kei-chan! That was one heck of a speech!!"
"Keiichi-kun... hau☆"
Mion gave me a solid clap on the shoulder. Rena was watching me from the corner of her eye, twisting and blushing and radiating bashful happiness.
Wh... what? Did I go too far? ...I'm kind of embarrassed myself now!
I reached for the teacup and lifted it to my mouth in one smooth motion.
"Soooo hoooooot!!!"
